diagram_title: Gmail MCP Managed App — A0 Context

activities.title: Authenticate Google Account
activities.description: OAuth connect flow: redirect to Google consent, exchange code for tokens, store to gauth.json, enable all installed Google apps

activities.title: Read Gmail Data
activities.description: List labels, search/list messages, get single message details, list drafts

activities.title: Compose Gmail Messages
activities.description: Send new message, reply to thread, forward message, create draft — all via RFC 2822 + base64url encoding

activities.title: Manage Gmail Labels
activities.description: Add/remove labels on messages, mark read/unread, star/unstar, move to trash

activities.title: Route Tool Execution
activities.description: MCP tool router dispatches AI tool calls to GmailApp executor, validates app readiness, formats Markdown output

arrows.label: GCP OAuth Client Config (CLIENT_ID, SECRET, SCOPES)
MCP Tool Router + Vercel AI SDK
Read Tool Dispatch (list-labels, list-messages, get-message, list-drafts)
Write Tool Dispatch (send-message, reply-message, forward-message, create-draft)

analysis_summary: Gmail MCP decomposes into 5 top-level functions: OAuth authentication, read operations, compose operations, label management, and tool routing. OAuth produces shared tokens consumed by all tool categories. Tool routing is the single entry point from AI sessions.
assumptions: Same GCP project serves both Calendar and Gmail OAuth
gauth.json is the single token store for all Google apps
Text/plain body extraction with snippet fallback is sufficient (no HTML rendering)
decision_trace: DD-1: Full scope https://mail.google.com/ per user requirement
DD-2: Shared gauth.json per user requirement
DD-3: OAuth connect merges scopes from all installed Google apps
